toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. Measure the wait-policy seam in iiac-perf: raw loop vs
   `_with(closure)` vs `_spin` must be zero-cost within
   noise before the endpoints build on it (the measurement
   clause of the shipped wait-policy hook) [[11]].
3. Descriptor queue endpoints: paired DescSender (loan +
   send) / DescReceiver (recv) owning ring endpoint +
   registry access, so the demo's ~20-line send path
   becomes ~3 lines and `resolve`'s unsafe is audited once
   inside the crate (recv safe by construction); guard
   handed back on Full [[11]].
4. Batch alloc/free demo: alongside the one-message
   alloc_free_1t loops, a variant that allocs X messages
   (5, 10, …) then frees them all, pool vs global
   allocator. We think the pool's rate stays constant
   (pop/push is O(1) regardless of live count, LIFO keeps
   the working set hot) while Box::new/drop slows as the
   batch outgrows malloc's thread-cache fast path — the
   demo should show it.
5. Endpoint claims word: CAS-claimed producer/consumer roles
   in the ring header so a second attach/split claimant gets
   an error instead of silently violating SPSC; costs a
   layout_version bump (or spends `_pad0`)
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#resolved-questions).
6. Typed endpoints: `Producer<T>` / `Consumer<T>` validating
   `T`'s geometry once at split instead of asserting on every
   reserve_slot_with [details](ring-buffer-design.md#api).

## Ideas

- Perf benches live in
  [iiac-perf](https://github.com/winksaville/iiac-perf)
  (sibling repo `../iiac-perf`), not here — its calibrated
  harness compares zc-ring against mpsc et al. directly
  (`zcring-1t`/`zcring-2t` mirroring `mpsc_1t`/`mpsc_2t`).
  An in-repo bench only if per-commit regression tracking
  proves necessary.

- Study [iceoryx2](https://github.com/eclipse-iceoryx/iceoryx2)
  before implementing message pools — battle-tested loan/send
  decoupling and pool-offset machinery; how it differs from
  this project in
  [Prior art: iceoryx2](ring-buffer-design.md#prior-art-iceoryx2).
- `#[global_allocator]` experiment over size-class pools:
  GlobalAlloc is `&self` + any-thread, so it needs
  shared-allocation pools (phase 2 gen-tagged head) or
  per-thread pools with a routing layer; arbitrary `Layout`
  needs size-class selection + an oversize fallback. Frees
  from any thread are already natural (MPSC push). Classic
  mempool → malloc arc; measure the object-pool form in
  iiac-perf first.
- Private per-handle cache in front of the shared free-stack
  (tcache-over-arenas): alloc/free hit a thread-private list
  with plain load/store; refill/flush moves batches to the
  CAS stack, amortizing one CAS over N messages. Motivating
  datum: 2 uncontended CAS = 8.7 ns of the pool's 9.9 ns
  single-thread round trip (vs malloc tcache's zero
  atomics). Hold until iiac-perf shows per-op CAS matters in
  a composed workload; we think the pool's tail latency
  (p99, stddev) already beats malloc — no arena locks, no
  brk/mmap — and that matters more than the mean.
- `Message` trait over the payload cast boilerplate: const
  `MSG_ID` + the zerocopy bounds; receiver-side dispatch
  (read tag, match, cast) without per-call-site ceremony,
  and maybe a transport seam so an embedded
  pointer-descriptor profile slots in behind the same API
  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#descriptor-and-registry-design-070).
- BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII, iceoryx2-style): kills the
  silent leak-on-drop footgun at the cost of guard-type
  asymmetry (ring guards' drop = do-nothing) and a
  ManuallyDrop dance in free/send paths. Decide when
  descriptor-queue send lands — explicit free is easier to
  upgrade than to walk back.
- Overflow FIFO: when a queue's ring is Full, append the
  message to a sender-private intrusive pending list (same
  embedded next-link the free-stack uses; zero allocation,
  naturally bounded by pool capacity)
  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#overflow-fifo-future).
- Blocking layer above the crate (futex, eventfd, async
  wakers) built on the header's user line — mechanism and
  contracts in
  [Blocking and user words](ring-buffer-design.md#blocking-and-user-words);
  possibly a companion wrapper crate so independent peers
  share one protocol.
- loom-based exhaustive ordering exploration of the SPSC
  protocol.
- Polish: `Error` implements `Display` + `core::error::Error`;
  `occupancy()` / `is_empty()` accessors.
- Packed-slot variant (drop the cache-line-multiple slot
  constraint) for small-message space efficiency.
- Per-target / configurable `CACHE_LINE` (128 for Apple
  M-series false sharing, tiny for cache-less MCUs) — safe
  since attach validates the header's `cache_line`; decide
  values from iiac-perf measurements.
- Embedded floor: protocol is atomic load/store only (no
  CAS), so thumbv6m works today; keep it that way where
  possible (endpoint claims wants CAS — gate it), and 8/16-bit
  targets would need index-width genericization.
- Shared `Geometry` struct (`slot_size`, `capacity`, `mask`)
  held by Ring and passed whole to the endpoint
  constructors, slimming their signatures and Ring's fields.
- Black-box test split: move the public-API protocol tests
  (roundtrip, abandoned guards, threaded stress) to
  `tests/protocol.rs`; white-box tests (u32 wrap, attach
  header internals) stay in lib.rs. Do it when a trybuild
  compile-fail harness lands there too (pins the
  "second reservation does not compile" guarantee).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

file.content:
rung: `reserve_slot_with(|_| false)` does everything it did
with identical loads, and its naive loop-on-the-fallible-call
usage carried a measured +30 ns / ~2× body jitter under
cross-core traffic (bug #1, whose raw-pattern benches live in
iiac-perf's `zcr-raw-*`). Removing it leaves a two-rung ladder
per endpoint — `reserve_slot_with` (fallible primitive) +
`reserve_slot_spin` (forever-spin convenience).

- `reserve_slot_with` is now the self-contained primitive: its
  doc absorbed the single-probe (`|_| false`) note and the
  raw-pointer / `>=`-corruption rationale the deleted method
  used to host.
- Call sites split by intent: single-probe (the `_1t` demos,
  the assertion tests, the README examples) →
  `reserve_slot_with(|_| false)`; loop-on-fallible spin waits
  (the `_2t` demos, the registry tests) → `reserve_slot_spin`.
- `threaded_spsc` keeps looping on `reserve_slot_with(|_| false)`
  so the fallible primitive still has a threaded-contention
  test distinct from `threaded_spsc_spin_variants` (which
  covers the spin rung).
- No `try_reserve_slot` alias yet: nothing outside tests makes
  `_with(|_| false)` common, so per bug #1 the alias waits for
  a real consumer.

### ring-buffer-design.md: blocking intro reconciled

The `## Blocking and user words` intro still read "the crate
never blocks and never spins" — accurate before the
wait-policy hook, but `reserve_slot_spin` spins now, so a bare
method rename would have left an inline contradiction. The
intro was reframed instead: the ring's fallible core never
blocks, and *how* to wait is injected policy (a shipped
`policy::spin` drives `reserve_slot_spin`); anything that
actually sleeps still belongs to the layer above via the
`user()` words. We think the rest of that section (the
`user()` wakeup-protocol contract) is unaffected by the API
change.

### As-built ladder

- `0.8.0` refactor: drop reserve_slot, keep _with ladder

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/zc-ring-x1/commit/32fec004bd30 "32fec004bd300cc072a052fd0f80882a582c790f"

input.new_string:
### As-built ladder

- `0.8.0` refactor: drop reserve_slot, keep _with ladder

## refactor: drop reserve_slot_spin and alloc_spin

Commits:

With `reserve_slot` gone, the endpoints' `reserve_slot_spin`
(and the pool's parallel `alloc_spin`) were the only remaining
`_spin` conveniences — each a three-line wrapper over
`*_with(policy::spin)` that hid the always-`Ok` unwrap. They
carry their weight only if callers reach for spin often; no
consumer outside the demo and tests does, so they go, leaving
one rung per operation (`*_with` + a shipped `policy::spin`
policy). `policy::spin` stays public — it is the migration
target and what the base demo lines now pass.

- Spin call sites migrate to `*_with(policy::spin).unwrap()`
  (`// OK: policy::spin never gives up` on non-test sites):
  the base 2t demos, the registry / lib / pool tests.
- `Pool::alloc_spin` removed for symmetry (todo named only
  `reserve_slot_spin`, but the same rationale applies); its
  lone test became `alloc_with_spin_returns_ok`.

### demo: the three-way wait-policy seam collapses

The demo carried a `raw` / `_closure` / `_spin` seam exemplar
— one workload in three wait forms, side by side, as an
eyeball zero-cost check. Two removals dissolved it:
`reserve_slot` (0.8.0) took the hand-rolled `raw` form, and
`reserve_slot_spin` (here) took `_spin`. What was left —
`_closure` (an inline spinning closure) vs the base line
(`policy::spin`) — is the same `reserve_slot_with` call twice
with a differently-spelled policy, so the `_closure` and
`_spin` demo fns were dropped as redundant. The base
`spsc_ring_one_msg_{1t,2t}` lines remain as the single
demonstration of the API. Calibrated seam measurement was
always iiac-perf's job (todo), not the demo's.

### README: spin benchmark blocks removed

The `zcr-spin-1t` / `zcr-spin-2t` measurement blocks named a
method that no longer exists. They were dropped rather than
relabeled; fresh iiac-perf numbers land in a later pass. The
`zcr-raw-*` / `zcr-with-*` blocks stay untouched.

# References

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file uses [Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
4	contains near term tasks with a short description and
5	uses links or reference links for more details.
6	
7	## In Progress
8	
9	_No cycle currently in progress._
10	
11	## Todo
12	
13	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
14	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
15	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
16	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
17	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
18	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
19	 number. Long-tail entries
20	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
21	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
22	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
23	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
24	
25	1. Measure the wait-policy seam in iiac-perf: raw loop vs
26	   `_with(closure)` vs `_spin` must be zero-cost within
27	   noise before the endpoints build on it (the measurement
28	   clause of the shipped wait-policy hook) [[11]].
29	2. Descriptor queue endpoints: paired DescSender (loan +
30	   send) / DescReceiver (recv) owning ring endpoint +
31	   registry access, so the demo's ~20-line send path
32	   becomes ~3 lines and `resolve`'s unsafe is audited once
33	   inside the crate (recv safe by construction); guard
34	   handed back on Full [[11]].
35	3. Batch alloc/free demo: alongside the one-message
36	   alloc_free_1t loops, a variant that allocs X messages
37	   (5, 10, …) then frees them all, pool vs global
38	   allocator. We think the pool's rate stays constant
39	   (pop/push is O(1) regardless of live count, LIFO keeps
40	   the working set hot) while Box::new/drop slows as the
41	   batch outgrows malloc's thread-cache fast path — the
42	   demo should show it.
43	4. Endpoint claims word: CAS-claimed producer/consumer roles
44	   in the ring header so a second attach/split claimant gets
45	   an error instead of silently violating SPSC; costs a
46	   layout_version bump (or spends `_pad0`)
47	   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#resolved-questions).
48	5. Typed endpoints: `Producer<T>` / `Consumer<T>` validating
49	   `T`'s geometry once at split instead of asserting on every
50	   reserve_slot_with [details](ring-buffer-design.md#api).
51	
52	## Ideas
53	
54	- Perf benches live in
55	  [iiac-perf](https://github.com/winksaville/iiac-perf)
56	  (sibling repo `../iiac-perf`), not here — its calibrated
57	  harness compares zc-ring against mpsc et al. directly
58	  (`zcring-1t`/`zcring-2t` mirroring `mpsc_1t`/`mpsc_2t`).
59	  An in-repo bench only if per-commit regression tracking
60	  proves necessary.
61	
62	- Study [iceoryx2](https://github.com/eclipse-iceoryx/iceoryx2)
63	  before implementing message pools — battle-tested loan/send
64	  decoupling and pool-offset machinery; how it differs from
65	  this project in
66	  [Prior art: iceoryx2](ring-buffer-design.md#prior-art-iceoryx2).
67	- `#[global_allocator]` experiment over size-class pools:
68	  GlobalAlloc is `&self` + any-thread, so it needs
69	  shared-allocation pools (phase 2 gen-tagged head) or
70	  per-thread pools with a routing layer; arbitrary `Layout`
71	  needs size-class selection + an oversize fallback. Frees
72	  from any thread are already natural (MPSC push). Classic
73	  mempool → malloc arc; measure the object-pool form in
74	  iiac-perf first.
75	- Private per-handle cache in front of the shared free-stack
76	  (tcache-over-arenas): alloc/free hit a thread-private list
77	  with plain load/store; refill/flush moves batches to the
78	  CAS stack, amortizing one CAS over N messages. Motivating
79	  datum: 2 uncontended CAS = 8.7 ns of the pool's 9.9 ns
80	  single-thread round trip (vs malloc tcache's zero
81	  atomics). Hold until iiac-perf shows per-op CAS matters in
82	  a composed workload; we think the pool's tail latency
83	  (p99, stddev) already beats malloc — no arena locks, no
84	  brk/mmap — and that matters more than the mean.
85	- `Message` trait over the payload cast boilerplate: const
86	  `MSG_ID` + the zerocopy bounds; receiver-side dispatch
87	  (read tag, match, cast) without per-call-site ceremony,
88	  and maybe a transport seam so an embedded
89	  pointer-descriptor profile slots in behind the same API
90	  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#descriptor-and-registry-design-070).
91	- BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII, iceoryx2-style): kills the
92	  silent leak-on-drop footgun at the cost of guard-type
93	  asymmetry (ring guards' drop = do-nothing) and a
94	  ManuallyDrop dance in free/send paths. Decide when
95	  descriptor-queue send lands — explicit free is easier to
96	  upgrade than to walk back.
97	- Overflow FIFO: when a queue's ring is Full, append the
98	  message to a sender-private intrusive pending list (same
99	  embedded next-link the free-stack uses; zero allocation,
100	  naturally bounded by pool capacity)
101	  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#overflow-fifo-future).
102	- Blocking layer above the crate (futex, eventfd, async
103	  wakers) built on the header's user line — mechanism and
104	  contracts in
105	  [Blocking and user words](ring-buffer-design.md#blocking-and-user-words);
106	  possibly a companion wrapper crate so independent peers
107	  share one protocol.
108	- loom-based exhaustive ordering exploration of the SPSC
109	  protocol.
110	- Polish: `Error` implements `Display` + `core::error::Error`;
111	  `occupancy()` / `is_empty()` accessors.
112	- Packed-slot variant (drop the cache-line-multiple slot
113	  constraint) for small-message space efficiency.
114	- Per-target / configurable `CACHE_LINE` (128 for Apple
115	  M-series false sharing, tiny for cache-less MCUs) — safe
116	  since attach validates the header's `cache_line`; decide
117	  values from iiac-perf measurements.
118	- Embedded floor: protocol is atomic load/store only (no
119	  CAS), so thumbv6m works today; keep it that way where
120	  possible (endpoint claims wants CAS — gate it), and 8/16-bit
121	  targets would need index-width genericization.
122	- Shared `Geometry` struct (`slot_size`, `capacity`, `mask`)
123	  held by Ring and passed whole to the endpoint
124	  constructors, slimming their signatures and Ring's fields.
125	- Black-box test split: move the public-API protocol tests
126	  (roundtrip, abandoned guards, threaded stress) to
127	  `tests/protocol.rs`; white-box tests (u32 wrap, attach
128	  header internals) stay in lib.rs. Do it when a trybuild
129	  compile-fail harness lands there too (pins the
130	  "second reservation does not compile" guarantee).

`reserve_slot_with(|_| false)` (identical loads, folds to the
same code), and its loop-on-fallible usage cost a measured
+30 ns / ~2x body jitter under cross-core traffic (bug #1).
Drop it for the two-rung ladder: `reserve_slot_with` (fallible
primitive) + `reserve_slot_spin` (forever-spin convenience).

- Cargo.toml: 0.7.3 -> 0.8.0
- src/consumer.rs: delete reserve_slot; reserve_slot_with is
  now the self-contained primitive (absorbs the |_| false
  single-probe note + raw-pointer rationale); repoint Empty,
  struct, reserve_slot_spin, and ReadSlot doc links
- src/producer.rs: same for the write side (Full, WriteSlot,
  the >=-corruption rationale)
- src/lib.rs: check_type doc; test single-probes ->
  _with(|_| false); threaded_spsc loops on _with(|_| false)
- src/registry.rs: test spin-loops -> reserve_slot_spin; drop
  now-unused Empty/Full imports
- src/pool.rs: alloc doc cross-reference
- src/bin/zc-ring-x1-demo.rs: _1t -> _with(|_| false), _2t ->
  reserve_slot_spin; module doc
- examples/readme.rs: _with(|_| false)
- README.md: API line + four snippets
- notes/bugs.md: remove fixed bug #1
- notes/todo.md: Done entry [[16]]; two incidental mentions
- notes/chores/chores-01.md: new chores section + blocking-intro
  reconciliation subsection; backfill seam-lines [[31]] and add +
  backfill the 35f144b section [[32]] (typo in its title kept to
  match the commit)
- notes/ring-buffer-design.md: API section -> two-rung ladder;
  reconcile the "never spins" blocking intro

reserve_slot_with(|_| false) or reserve_slot_spin.
